approach:
Two approaches exist: JUNK (full SvelteKit app with multi-tab UI, session management, agents) vs JUNK2 (minimal worker-served HTML with xterm.js). Current project focuses on backend WebSocket reliability first, then frontend.

CURRENT STATUS: BLOCKED - See BUG-REPORT-WebSocket-Message-Flow.md for critical bug blocking all WebSocket functionality. Worker serves HTML and WebSocket endpoint, but container server cannot establish reliable connection to local ttyd process, causing terminals to appear black.

stories.title: Verify basic sandbox connectivity
stories.description: Confirm we can create a sandbox instance and make HTTP requests to it. No WebSocket yet - just prove the container starts and responds.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox instance logs show container started

stories.description: Confirm we can create a sandbox instance and make HTTP requests to it. No WebSocket yet - just prove the container starts and responds.
acceptance_criteria: Sandbox instance logs show container started

stories.title: Start ttyd process in sandbox
stories.description: Use sandbox.startProcess() to launch ttyd on port 7681. Verify it starts and port becomes available.

description: Use sandbox.startProcess() to launch ttyd on port 7681. Verify it starts and port becomes available.

title: Establish WebSocket to ttyd
blocking_issue: Depends on WS-002 - container must successfully start ttyd and connect to it
description: Use sandbox.wsConnect() to establish WebSocket connection to ttyd. Verify 101 upgrade response with webSocket property.
Use sandbox.wsConnect() to establish WebSocket connection to ttyd. Verify 101 upgrade response with webSocket property.

verification_command.title: Bidirectional message flow
verification_command.blocking_issue: Depends on WS-003 - container must connect to ttyd and establish message flow
verification_command.description: Verify messages flow both directions: client→ttyd and ttyd→client. Send terminal size, receive prompt.
verification_command.verification_command: manual test with websocat or browser

description: Verify messages flow both directions: client→ttyd and ttyd→client. Send terminal size, receive prompt.
verification_command: manual test with websocat or browser

verification_command.title: Add Effect-based error handling
verification_command.blocking_issue: Depends on WS-004 - message flow must work before error handling can be tested
verification_command.description: Wrap sandbox operations in Effect for typed errors, retries, and timeouts. Reference ~/.vendor/effect patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Retry with exponential backoff on transient failures
verification_command.verification_command: bun run dev && manually trigger error scenarios

description: Wrap sandbox operations in Effect for typed errors, retries, and timeouts. Reference ~/.vendor/effect patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Retry with exponential backoff on transient failures
verification_command: bun run dev && manually trigger error scenarios

verification_command.title: Production deploy and verify
verification_command.blocking_issue: Depends on all WebSocket stories (WS-001 through WS-005) working end-to-end
verification_command.description: Deploy to prod (api.myfilepath.com) and verify same behavior as local dev.

description: Deploy to prod (api.myfilepath.com) and verify same behavior as local dev.

verification_command.title: Minimal frontend - worker-served HTML with xterm.js
verification_command.blocking_issue: WebSocket endpoint (/terminal/:id/ws) does not work end-to-end - terminals remain black
verification_command.description: Add simple HTML page served from worker (like JUNK2 approach) with xterm.js loaded via CDN. Single terminal, no tabs, no session management.
acceptance_criteria: GET / returns HTML page with xterm.js terminal
Terminal displays bash prompt and accepts input
Works in both local dev and production

description: Add simple HTML page served from worker (like JUNK2 approach) with xterm.js loaded via CDN. Single terminal, no tabs, no session management.
acceptance_criteria: GET / returns HTML page with xterm.js terminal

verification_command.title: Full SvelteKit frontend (reference JUNK and JUNK2)
verification_command.description: Build full SvelteKit app with multi-tab UI, session management, agent selection (like JUNK and JUNK2). This is the full experience but should be done after minimal frontend works. This is the middle of the deploy goal.
acceptance_criteria: Real-time tab state sync via WebSocket
verification_command.verification_command: bun run dev && test full UI flow
